title: Coppensia discifera
intro: Coppensia discifera, also known as Borreria, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents present in Coppensia discifera include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Coppensia discifera include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Coppensia discifera is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to South Africa. Coppensia discifera belongs to the tribe Inuleae, and further classification can be found within the subfamily Asteroideae. The plant is a part of the larger classification system known as Angiosperms, which includes flowering plants. Further classification may include the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida.
